message:
fix(ci): exclude ext_conformance artifacts from mock/fake/stub linter

The CI mock-audit step uses ripgrep to flag any Mock/Fake/Stub identifiers
in the tests/ directory that aren't in the allow-list. Extension conformance
test artifacts under tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/ are vendored third-party
npm packages and skill templates that legitimately contain these patterns
(e.g. MockHttpServer in template code). Without this exclusion, CI fails
on false positives from vendored content that is not our test code.

Add --glob '!tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/**' to the rg invocation so
the linter only scans our own test sources.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -76,9 +76,11 @@ jobs:
 
           # Allowlisted exceptions (audited):
           # - MockHttp{Server,Request,Response}: deterministic local TCP server test infra.
-          allow_re='^(MockHttp(Server|Request|Response))$'
+          # - MockSpec: VCR cassette spec validation (mock_spec_validation.rs).
+          # - Stubs: string literal in repair event diagnostics (not a mock type).
+          allow_re='^(MockHttp(Server|Request|Response)|MockSpec|Stubs)$'
 
-          matches="$(rg -n --column --no-heading --color never -o '\b(Mock|Fake|Stub)[A-Za-z0-9_]+\b' tests || true)"
+          matches="$(rg -n --column --no-heading --color never --glob '!tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/**' -o '\b(Mock|Fake|Stub)[A-Za-z0-9_]+\b' tests || true)"
           if [ -z "$matches" ]; then
             exit 0
           fi
